+package config
+
+import (
+	"testing"
+
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/cache"
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/maps"
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/runtime/mock"
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/segments"
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/template"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
+)
+
+func TestSegmentCache(t *testing.T) {
+	template.Cache = &cache.Template{
+		Segments: maps.NewConcurrent[any](),
+	}
+	defer cache.DeleteAll(cache.Device)
+
+	env := new(mock.Environment)
+	env.On("Pwd").Return("/tmp")
+
+	segment := &Segment{
+		Type: TEXT,
+		Cache: &Cache{
+			Strategy: Folder,
+			Duration: cache.Duration("10m"),
+		},
+		Alias: "my_text_segment",
+		env:   env,
+	}
+
+	textWriter := &segments.Text{}
+	textWriter.Init(nil, nil)
+	textWriter.SetText("Hello, Cache!")
+	segment.writer = textWriter
+	segment.name = "my_text_segment"
+
+	segment.setCache()
+
+	newSegment := &Segment{
+		Type: TEXT,
+		Cache: &Cache{
+			Strategy: Folder,
+			Duration: cache.Duration("10m"),
+		},
+		Alias: "my_text_segment",
+		env:   env,
+	}
+	newSegment.name = "my_text_segment"
+
+	newTextWriter := &segments.Text{}
+	newTextWriter.Init(nil, nil)
+	newSegment.writer = newTextWriter
+
+	restored := newSegment.restoreCache()
+
+	assert.True(t, restored, "Cache should be restored")
+	assert.NotNil(t, newSegment.writer, "Writer should be restored")
+	assert.IsType(t, &segments.Text{}, newSegment.writer, "Writer should be of type *segments.Text")
+	if newSegment.writer != nil {
+		assert.Equal(t, "Hello, Cache!", newSegment.writer.Text(), "Restored text should match")
+	}
+
+	// Test legacy cache (string value)
+	legacySegment := &Segment{
+		Type: TEXT,
+		Cache: &Cache{
+			Strategy: Device,
+			Duration: cache.Duration("10m"),
+		},
+		Alias: "legacy_segment",
+		env:   env,
+	}
+	legacySegment.name = "legacy_segment"
+
+	// Initialize writer so cacheKeyAndStore can compute the key
+	legacyWriter := &segments.Text{}
+	legacyWriter.Init(nil, nil)
+	legacySegment.writer = legacyWriter
+
+	// Derive the key using the same logic as production code
+	legacyKey, legacyStore := legacySegment.cacheKeyAndStore()
+	cache.Set(legacyStore, legacyKey, "legacy_json_string", cache.Duration("10m"))
+
+	// Should return false and delete the key
+	restoredLegacy := legacySegment.restoreCache()
+	assert.False(t, restoredLegacy, "Legacy cache should not be restored")
+
+	// Verify key is gone
+	_, found := cache.Get[string](legacyStore, legacyKey)
+	assert.False(t, found, "Legacy key should be removed")
+}

94	type segmentAlias Segment

102	func (segment *Segment) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
103		aux := &segmentAux{
104			segmentAlias: (*segmentAlias)(segment),
105		}
106	
107		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, aux); err != nil {
108			return err
109		}
110	
111		// Migrate 'properties' to 'options' if present
112		if len(aux.Properties) > 0 && len(segment.Options) == 0 {
113			segment.Options = aux.Properties
114		}
115	
116		return nil
117	}

119	func (segment *Segment) UnmarshalYAML(node *yaml.Node) error {
120		// Decode into a map to handle field renaming
121		var raw map[string]any
122		if err := node.Decode(&raw); err != nil {
123			return err
124		}
125	
126		// If 'properties' exists and 'options' doesn't, rename it
127		if props, hasProps := raw["properties"]; hasProps {
128			if _, hasOptions := raw["options"]; !hasOptions {
129				raw["options"] = props
130				delete(raw, "properties")
131			}
132		}
133	
134		// Re-encode and decode into the struct
135		modifiedNode := &yaml.Node{}
136		if err := modifiedNode.Encode(raw); err != nil {
137			return err
138		}
139	
140		return modifiedNode.Decode((*segmentAlias)(segment))
141	}

166	func (segment *Segment) Execute(env runtime.Environment) {
167		// segment timings for debug purposes
168		var start time.Time
169		if env.Flags().Debug {
170			start = time.Now()
171			segment.NameLength = len(segment.Name())
172			defer func() {
173				segment.Duration = time.Since(start)
174			}()
175		}
176	
177		defer segment.evaluateNeeds()
178	
179		err := segment.MapSegmentWithWriter(env)
180		if err != nil || !segment.shouldIncludeFolder() {
181			return
182		}
183	
184		log.Debugf("segment: %s", segment.Name())
185	
186		if segment.isToggled() {
187			return
188		}
189	
190		if segment.restoreData() {
191			return
192		}
193	
194		cacheRestored := segment.restoreCache()
195		if cacheRestored && !env.Flags().Streaming {
196			return
197		}
198	
199		if shouldHideForWidth(segment.env, segment.MinWidth, segment.MaxWidth) {
200			return
201		}
202	
203		defer func() {
204			if segment.Enabled {
205				template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
206			}
207		}()
208	
209		// Only segments with a timeout can ever be killed via
210		// KillGoroutineChildren (see prompt/segments.go executeSegmentWithTimeout),
211		// so only those need a Job object to track/terminate their child
212		// processes. Skipping this for the common case (no timeout configured)
213		// avoids two syscalls + a map insert per segment per prompt.
214		if segment.Timeout > 0 {
215			if err := runjobs.CreateJobForGoroutine(segment.Name()); err != nil {
216				log.Errorf("failed to create job for goroutine (segment: %s): %v", segment.Name(), err)
217			}
218	
219			// Release the Job object (Windows) once this goroutine is done
220			// spawning/waiting on children. cmd.Run blocks until its child exits,
221			// so by the time Execute returns - on any path below, including a
222			// panic unwind - no process we intended to keep alive is still
223			// assigned to the job, making it safe to close despite
224			// JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE. If the timeout path already
225			// terminated/closed the job concurrently, this is a no-op; see the
226			// ownership invariant documented on jobs.CloseGoroutineJob.
227			defer runjobs.CloseGoroutineJob()

234	func (segment *Segment) Render(index int, force bool) bool {
235		// Allow pending segments to render (they'll show "..." text)
236		if !segment.Pending && !segment.Enabled && !force {
237			return segment.renderFallback(index)
238		}
239	
240		if force {
241			segment.Force = true
242		}
243	
244		// Foreground/background may be overridden directly (e.g. color cycling) between
245		// render passes, so the memoized values must not survive across calls to Render.
246		segment.foregroundResolved = false
247		segment.backgroundResolved = false
248	
249		segment.writer.SetIndex(index)
250	
251		text := segment.string()
252	
253		// Only update Enabled if segment is NOT pending (avoid race with Execute goroutine)
254		if !segment.Pending {
255			segment.Enabled = segment.Force || strings.ContainsFunc(text, func(r rune) bool { return r != ' ' })
256	
257			if !segment.Enabled {
258				template.Cache.RemoveSegmentData(segment.Name())
259				return false
260			}
261		}
262	
263		segment.SetText(text)
264		segment.setCache()
265	
266		// We do this to make `.Text` available for a cross-segment reference in an extra prompt.
267		template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
268	
269		return true
270	}
271	
272	// renderFallback attempts to render FallbackTemplate when a segment would
273	// otherwise be hidden because its writer's Enabled() returned false. It
274	// requires the writer to have completed its evaluation; segments hidden for other
275	// reasons (toggled off, folder include/exclude, width constraints, or a
276	// writer mapping error) never set evaluated, so they keep the silent-omission
277	// behavior. Segments killed by their timeout are excluded via Killed rather
278	// than evaluated, because their Execute goroutine keeps running after the
279	// kill and may still complete the evaluation before rendering starts.
280	func (segment *Segment) renderFallback(index int) bool {
281		if segment.FallbackTemplate == "" || !segment.evaluated || segment.Killed {
282			return false
283		}
284	
285		text, err := template.Render(segment.FallbackTemplate, segment.writer)
286		if err != nil {
287			text = err.Error()
288		}
289	
290		if !strings.ContainsFunc(text, func(r rune) bool { return r != ' ' }) {
291			return false
292		}
293	
294		// Foreground/background may be overridden directly (e.g. color cycling) between
295		// render passes, so the memoized values must not survive across calls to Render.
296		segment.foregroundResolved = false
297		segment.backgroundResolved = false
298	
299		segment.writer.SetIndex(index)
300		segment.Enabled = true
301		segment.SetText(text)
302	
303		// Intentionally skip setCache(): the writer is zero/partially hydrated
304		// here, and caching it would make restoreCache() later resurrect a
305		// disabled writer as enabled while rendering the main template against
306		// empty data.
307		template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
308	
309		return true
310	}

409	func (segment *Segment) restoreCache() bool {
410		if !segment.hasCache() {
411			return false
412		}
413	
414		key, store := segment.cacheKeyAndStore()
415	
416		data, OK := cache.Get[any](store, key)
417		if !OK {
418			log.Debugf("no cache found for segment: %s, key: %s", segment.Name(), key)
419			return false
420		}
421	
422		switch v := data.(type) {
423		case SegmentWriter:
424			segment.writer = v
425			segment.Enabled = true
426			template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
427			log.Debug("restored segment from cache: ", segment.Name())
428			segment.restored = true
429			return true
430		case string:
431			// legacy JSON cache entry, remove it so it gets re-cached with the new format
432			log.Debugf("removing legacy cache key: %s", key)
433			cache.Delete(store, key)
434			return false
435		default:
436			log.Debugf("unexpected cache type for segment: %s, key: %s", segment.Name(), key)
437			cache.Delete(store, key)
438			return false
439		}
440	}
441	
442	// restoreData replays a segment's writer state from the data file supplied via
443	// runtime.Flags.SegmentData, bypassing the real runtime entirely. This lets
444	// segments render from a recorded fixture instead of probing the environment.
445	func (segment *Segment) restoreData() bool {
446		data, OK := segment.env.Flags().SegmentData[segment.DataKey()]
447		if !OK {
448			return false
449		}
450	
451		err := json.Unmarshal(data, &segment.writer)
452		if err != nil {
453			log.Error(err)
454			return false
455		}
456	
457		segment.Enabled = true
458		segment.restored = true
459	
460		template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
461	
462		log.Debug("restored segment from data: ", segment.Name())
463	
464		return true
465	}
466	
467	func (segment *Segment) setCache() {
468		if segment.restored || !segment.hasCache() {
469			return
470		}
471	
472		// Never cache pending state to avoid polluting cache with incomplete data
473		if segment.Pending {
474			return
475		}
476	
477		// TODO: check if we can make segmentwriter a generic Type indicator
478		// that way we can actually get the value straight from cache.Get
479		// and marchalling is obsolete
480		key, store := segment.cacheKeyAndStore()
481		cache.Set(store, key, segment.writer, segment.Cache.Duration)
482	}
483	
484	func (segment *Segment) cacheKeyAndStore() (string, cache.Store) {
485		format := "segment_cache_%s"
486		switch segment.Cache.Strategy {
487		case Session:
488			return fmt.Sprintf(format, segment.Name()), cache.Session
489		case Device:
490			return fmt.Sprintf(format, segment.Name()), cache.Device
491		case Folder:
492			fallthrough
493		default:
494			return fmt.Sprintf(format, strings.Join([]string{segment.Name(), segment.folderKey()}, "_")), cache.Device
495		}
496	}
497	
498	func (segment *Segment) folderKey() string {
499		key, ok := segment.writer.CacheKey()
500		if !ok {
501			return segment.env.Pwd()
502		}
503	
504		return key
505	}
506	
507	func (segment *Segment) string() string {
508		// Use simple pending text if segment is still pending
509		if segment.Pending {
510			if segment.Placeholder != "" {
511				return segment.Placeholder
512			}
513	
514			return "..."
515		}
516	
517		result := segment.Templates.Resolve(segment.writer, "", segment.TemplatesLogic)
518		if len(result) != 0 {
519			return result
520		}
521	
522		if segment.Template == "" {
523			segment.Template = segment.writer.Template()
524		}
525	
526		text, err := template.Render(segment.Template, segment.writer)
527		if err != nil {
528			return err.Error()
529		}
530	
531		return text
532	}
533	
534	func (segment *Segment) shouldIncludeFolder() bool {
535		if segment.env == nil {
536			return true
537		}
538	
539		cwdIncluded := segment.cwdIncluded()
540		cwdExcluded := segment.cwdExcluded()
541	
542		return cwdIncluded && !cwdExcluded
543	}
544	
545	func (segment *Segment) cwdIncluded() bool {
546		if len(segment.IncludeFolders) == 0 {
547			return true
548		}
549	
550		return segment.env.DirMatchesOneOf(segment.env.Pwd(), segment.IncludeFolders)
551	}
552	
553	func (segment *Segment) cwdExcluded() bool {
554		return segment.env.DirMatchesOneOf(segment.env.Pwd(), segment.ExcludeFolders)
555	}
556	
557	func (segment *Segment) evaluateNeeds() {
558		if segment.needsEvaluated {
559			return
560		}
561	
562		segment.needsEvaluated = true
563	
564		value := segment.Template
565		if value == "" && segment.writer != nil {
566			value = segment.writer.Template()
567		}
568	
569		if len(segment.ForegroundTemplates) != 0 {
570			value += strings.Join(segment.ForegroundTemplates, "")
571		}
572	
573		if len(segment.BackgroundTemplates) != 0 {
574			value += strings.Join(segment.BackgroundTemplates, "")
575		}
576	
577		if len(segment.Templates) != 0 {
578			value += strings.Join(segment.Templates, "")
579		}
580	
581		if segment.FallbackTemplate != "" {
582			value += segment.FallbackTemplate
583		}
584	
585		if !strings.Contains(value, ".Segments.") {
586			return
587		}
588	
589		matches := regex.FindAllNamedRegexMatch(`\.Segments\.(?P<NAME>[a-zA-Z0-9]+)`, value)
590		for _, name := range matches {
591			segmentName := name["NAME"]
592	
593			if len(name) == 0 || slices.Contains(segment.Needs, segmentName) {
594				continue
595			}
596	
597			segment.Needs = append(segment.Needs, segmentName)
598		}
599	}
600	
601	func (segment *Segment) key() any {
602		if segment.Index > 0 {
603			return segment.Index - 1
604		}
605	
606		return segment.Name()
607	}

3	import (
4		"encoding/gob"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// ErrLocked is returned by openFile when the cache file is held exclusively
10	// by another process (e.g. a Windows sharing violation that persisted past
11	// the retry window). Callers must treat this as "leave the file alone":
12	// operate purely in-memory for this run and do not recreate/truncate the
13	// file on close.
14	var ErrLocked = errors.New("cache file is locked by another process")
15	
16	func init() {
17		gob.Register(&Entry[any]{})
18		gob.Register(Template{})
19		gob.Register(SimpleTemplate{})
20		gob.Register((*Duration)(nil))
21		gob.Register(map[string]bool{})
22		gob.Register(commandPathEntry{})
23	}
24	
25	const (
26		DeviceStore = "omp.cache"
27	)
28	
29	const (
30		TEMPLATECACHE    = "template_cache"
31		TOGGLECACHE      = "toggle_cache"
32		PROMPTCOUNTCACHE = "prompt_count_cache"
33		ENGINECACHE      = "engine_cache"
34		FONTLISTCACHE    = "font_list_cache"
35		CLAUDECACHE      = "claude_cache"
36		COPILOTCLICACHE  = "copilot_cli_cache"
37	)
38	
39	type Entry[T any] struct {
40		Value     T
41		Timestamp int64
42		TTL       int
43	}
44	
45	func (c *Entry[T]) Expired() bool {
46		if c.TTL < 0 {
47			return false
48		}
49	
50		return time.Now().Unix() >= (c.Timestamp + int64(c.TTL))
51	}

1	package cache
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/gob"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strings"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/log"
13		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/maps"
14	)
15	
16	type store struct {
17		cache    *maps.Concurrent[*Entry[any]]
18		filePath string
19		dirty    bool
20		persist  bool
21		// locked is set when the on-disk cache file could not be opened because
22		// another process holds it (e.g. Windows sharing violation that
23		// persisted past the retry window). When true, the store operates
24		// purely in-memory for the lifetime of this process: close() must not
25		// attempt to (re)create the file, since doing so would truncate the
26		// other process's data.
27		locked bool
28	}
29	
30	var (
31		session *store
32		device  *store
33	)
34	
35	type Store string
36	
37	const (
38		Session Store  = "session"
39		Device  Store  = "device"
40		TTL     string = "ttl"
41	)
42	
43	func (s Store) new() *store {
44		return &store{
45			cache: maps.NewConcurrent[*Entry[any]](),
46		}
47	}
48	
49	// getStore returns the appropriate store based on the Store identifier
50	func (s Store) get() *store {
51		switch s {
52		case Device:
53			if device == nil {
54				device = s.new()
55			}
56	
57			return device
58		default:
59			if session == nil {
60				session = s.new()
61			}
62	
63			return session
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// Init initializes a store with the given file path
68	func (s Store) init(filePath string, persist bool) {
69		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s), filePath)
70	
71		store := s.get()
72		store.cache = maps.NewConcurrent[*Entry[any]]()
73		store.filePath = filepath.Join(Path(), filePath)
74		store.persist = persist
75		store.dirty = false
76		store.locked = false
77	
78		reader, err := openFile(store.filePath)
79		if err != nil {
80			if errors.Is(err, ErrLocked) {
81				// Another process holds the file. Leave it alone: run this
82				// session purely in-memory, don't mark dirty, don't recreate
83				// on close.
84				log.Debugf("(%s) cache file is locked, running in-memory only", string(s))
85				store.locked = true
86				return
87			}
88	
89			// set to dirty so we create it on close
90			log.Error(err)
91			store.dirty = true
92			return
93		}
94	
95		defer reader.Close()
96	
97		var list maps.Simple[*Entry[any]]
98	
99		dec := gob.NewDecoder(reader)
100		if err := dec.Decode(&list); err != nil {
101			log.Error(err)
102			// If gob decoding fails, the cache file might be from the old format
103			// Set dirty to true so we recreate it in gob format
104			store.dirty = true
105			return
106		}
107	
108		for key, entry := range list {
109			if entry.Expired() {
110				log.Debugf("(%s) skipping expired key: %s", string(s), key)
111				continue
112			}
113	
114			log.Debugf("(%s) loading %s", string(s), key)
115			store.cache.Set(key, entry)
116		}
117	}
118	
119	// touchSessionFile updates the session file's modification time if it's older than 1 hour.
120	// This prevents stale session cache files from being cleaned up while reducing steady-state overhead.
121	func touchSessionFile(filePath string) {
122		info, err := os.Stat(filePath)
123		if err != nil {
124			return
125		}
126	
127		if time.Since(info.ModTime()) <= time.Hour {
128			return
129		}
130	
131		if err := os.Chtimes(filePath, time.Now(), time.Now()); err != nil {
132			log.Error(err)
133		}
134	}
135	
136	func (s Store) close() {
137		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s))
138	
139		store := s.get()
140		if store == nil || store.locked || !store.persist || !store.dirty {
141			if s == Session && store != nil && !store.locked && store.filePath != "" {
142				touchSessionFile(store.filePath)
143			}
144	
145			log.Debugf("(%s) not persisting", string(s))
146			return
147		}
148	
149		cache := store.cache.ToSimple()
150	
151		file, err := openFileForWrite(store.filePath)
152		if err != nil {
153			if errors.Is(err, ErrLocked) {
154				// Became locked between init and close (e.g. another process
155				// started writing meanwhile) — do not recreate/truncate.
156				log.Debugf("(%s) cache file locked on close, skipping persist", string(s))
157				return
158			}
159	
160			log.Error(err)
161			return
162		}
163	
164		defer func() {
165			if err := file.Close(); err != nil {
166				log.Error(err)
167			}
168		}()
169	
170		enc := gob.NewEncoder(file)
171		if err := enc.Encode(cache); err != nil {
172			log.Error(err)
173		}
174	}
175	
176	// Get retrieves a typed value from the specified store
177	func Get[T any](s Store, key string) (T, bool) {
178		var zero T
179		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s), key)
180	
181		store := s.get()
182		if store == nil {
183			log.Debugf("(%s) store is nil", string(s))
184			return zero, false
185		}
186	
187		entry, found := store.cache.Get(key)
188		if !found {
189			log.Debugf("(%s) key not found: %s", string(s), key)
190			return zero, false
191		}
192	
193		if entry.Expired() {
194			log.Debugf("(%s) key expired: %s", string(s), key)
195			store.cache.Delete(key)
196			store.dirty = true
197			return zero, false
198		}
199	
200		// Type assertion to get the typed value
201		if typed, ok := entry.Value.(T); ok {
202			log.Debugf("(%s) found entry: %s - %v", string(s), key, typed)
203			return typed, true
204		}
205	
206		log.Error(fmt.Errorf("(%s) type mismatch for key: %s. Got %T, expected %T", string(s), key, entry.Value, zero))
207		return zero, false
208	}
209	
210	// Set stores a typed value in the specified store
211	func Set[T any](s Store, key string, value T, duration Duration) {
212		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s), key)
213	
214		store := s.get()
215		if store == nil {
216			log.Debugf("(%s) store is nil", string(s))
217			return
218		}
219	
220		seconds := duration.Seconds()
221		if seconds == 0 {
222			return
223		}
224	
225		log.Debugf("(%s) setting entry: %s - %v with duration: %s", string(s), key, value, string(duration))
226	
227		store.cache.Set(key, &Entry[any]{
228			Value:     value,
229			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
230			TTL:       seconds,
231		})
232	
233		store.dirty = true
234	}
235	
236	// Delete removes a key from the specified store
237	func Delete(s Store, key string) {
238		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s), key)
239	
240		store := s.get()
241		if store == nil {
242			log.Debugf("(%s) store is nil", string(s))
243			return
244		}
245	
246		log.Debugf("(%s) deleting key: %s", string(s), key)
247		store.cache.Delete(key)
248		store.dirty = true
249	}
250	
251	func DeleteAll(s Store) {
252		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s))
253	
254		store := s.get()
255		if store == nil {
256			log.Debugf("(%s) store is nil", string(s))
257			return
258		}
259	
260		store.cache = maps.NewConcurrent[*Entry[any]]()
261		store.dirty = true
262	}
263	
264	func Print(s Store) string {
265		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s))
266	
267		store := s.get()
268		if store == nil {
269			return fmt.Sprintf("Store %s is nil", string(s))
270		}
271	
272		cache := store.cache.ToSimple()
273		if len(cache) == 0 {
274			return fmt.Sprintf("Store %s is empty", string(s))
275		}
276	
277		var builder strings.Builder
278	
279		for key, entry := range cache {
280			builder.WriteString("\n")
281	
282			if entry.Expired() {
283				fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Key: %s [EXPIRED]\n", key)
284				builder.WriteString("\n")
285				continue
286			}
287	
288			var ttlInfo string
289			if entry.TTL < 0 {
290				ttlInfo = "never expires"
291			}
292			if entry.TTL >= 0 {
293				expiresAt := time.Unix(entry.Timestamp+int64(entry.TTL), 0)
294				ttlInfo = fmt.Sprintf("expires at %s", expiresAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05"))
295			}
296	
297			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Key: %s\n", key)
298			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "  Value: %s\n", fmt.Sprintf("%#v", entry.Value))
299			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "  Type: %T\n", entry.Value)
300			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "  Created: %s\n", time.Unix(entry.Timestamp, 0).Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05"))
301			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "  TTL: %s\n", ttlInfo)
302		}
303	
304		return builder.String()
305	}

31	func loadCache(vars maps.Simple[any], aliases *maps.Config) {
32		if !env.Flags().IsPrimary {
33			// Load the template cache for a non-primary prompt before rendering any templates.
34			if OK := restoreCache(); OK {
35				// Interrupted is a per-invocation flag, never a cached value: the
36				// restored blob was saved by the primary prompt (never interrupted),
37				// so it must be refreshed from the current flags.
38				Cache.Interrupted = env.Flags().Interrupted
39				return
40			}
41		}
42	
43		// Build fully before assigning: a long-lived process (serve) rebuilds
44		// this per prompt while goroutines from an abandoned render cycle may
45		// still read the global - they must only ever observe a complete object.
46		tmpl := new(cache.Template)
47	
48		tmpl.Root = env.Root()
49		tmpl.Shell = env.Shell()
50		tmpl.ShellVersion = env.Flags().ShellVersion
51		tmpl.Code, _ = env.StatusCodes()
52		tmpl.Interrupted = env.Flags().Interrupted
53		tmpl.WSL = env.IsWsl()
54		tmpl.Segments = maps.NewConcurrent[any]()
55		tmpl.PromptCount = env.Flags().PromptCount
56		tmpl.Var = make(map[string]any)
57		tmpl.Jobs = env.Flags().JobCount
58		tmpl.Version = build.Version
59	
60		if vars != nil {
61			tmpl.Var = vars
62		}
63	
64		pwd := env.Pwd()
65		tmpl.PWD = path.ReplaceHomeDirPrefixWithTilde(pwd)
66	
67		tmpl.AbsolutePWD = pwd
68		if env.IsWsl() {
69			tmpl.AbsolutePWD, _ = env.RunCommand("wslpath", "-m", pwd)
70		}
71	
72		env.Flags().AbsolutePWD = tmpl.AbsolutePWD
73		tmpl.PSWD = env.Flags().PSWD
74	
75		tmpl.Folder = path.Base(pwd)
76		if env.GOOS() == runtime.WINDOWS && strings.HasSuffix(tmpl.Folder, ":") {
77			tmpl.Folder += `\`
78		}
79	
80		tmpl.UserName = env.User()
81		if host, err := env.Host(); err == nil {
82			tmpl.HostName = host
83		}
84	
85		goos := env.GOOS()
86		tmpl.OS = goos
87		if goos == runtime.LINUX {
88			tmpl.OS = env.Platform()
89		}
90	
91		val := env.Getenv("SHLVL")
92		if shlvl, err := strconv.Atoi(val); err == nil {
93			tmpl.SHLVL = shlvl
94		}
95	
96		overlayEnvData(tmpl)
97	
98		// Alias mapping must apply to a data-provided value exactly as it does to
99		// a live one, so it runs after the overlay, on whichever value won.
100		tmpl.Shell = aliases.GetShellName(tmpl.Shell)
101		tmpl.UserName = aliases.GetUserName(tmpl.UserName)
102		tmpl.HostName = aliases.GetHostName(tmpl.HostName)
103	
104		Cache = tmpl
105	}
106	
107	// overlayEnvData merges the data file's env section onto an already-built
108	// template cache. It only touches keys present in the data, so anything not
109	// specified keeps its live value. Keys the CLI layer already routed into
110	// runtime.Flags (see routedEnvDataKeys) are stripped first so a raw file
111	// value can never clobber the flag-precedence-resolved value. Failures are
112	// logged, never fatal - the prompt renders with live values regardless.
113	func overlayEnvData(tmpl *cache.Template) {
114		data := env.Flags().EnvData
115		if len(data) == 0 {
116			return
117		}
118	
119		var fields map[string]json.RawMessage
120		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &fields); err != nil {
121			log.Error(err)
122			return
123		}
124	
125		for _, key := range routedEnvDataKeys {
126			delete(fields, key)
127		}
128	
129		// SegmentsCache/Segments are internal cache plumbing, not user-facing
130		// template data; a data file must never be able to overwrite them. Var
131		// (template vars) is left alone - overriding it from the data file is
132		// intentional and useful.
133		delete(fields, "SegmentsCache")
134		delete(fields, "Segments")
135	
136		overlay, err := json.Marshal(fields)
137		if err != nil {
138			log.Error(err)
139			return
140		}
141	
142		if err := json.Unmarshal(overlay, &tmpl.SimpleTemplate); err != nil {
143			log.Error(err)
144		}
145	}
146	
147	func restoreCache() bool {
148		defer log.Trace(time.Now())
149	
150		val, OK := cache.Get[cache.SimpleTemplate](cache.Session, cache.TEMPLATECACHE)
151		if !OK {
152			return false
153		}
154	
155		Cache = new(cache.Template)
156		Cache.SimpleTemplate = val
157		Cache.Segments = Cache.SegmentsCache.ToConcurrent()
158	
159		return true
160	}
161	
162	func SaveCache() {
163		// only store this when in a primary prompt
164		// and when we have any extra prompt in the config
165		canSave := env.Flags().IsPrimary && env.Flags().HasExtra
166		if !canSave {
167			return
168		}
169	
170		Cache.SegmentsCache = Cache.Segments.ToSimple()
171	
172		cache.Set(cache.Session, cache.TEMPLATECACHE, &Cache.SimpleTemplate, cache.ONEDAY)
173	}

50	// It encodes the raw (unpatched) template text and, when context is non-nil,
51	// the reflect.Type of the context so that two different struct types whose
52	// patchTemplate output would differ (via hasField) get separate cache entries.
53	// For map[string]any contexts the key also includes the sorted exported key
54	// names, since patchTemplate output depends on which keys are present.
55	func templateCacheKey(rawText string, ctx any) string {
56		if ctx == nil {
57			return rawText
58		}
59	
60		t := reflect.TypeOf(ctx)
61		if t.Kind() == reflect.Map {
62			if m, ok := ctx.(map[string]any); ok {
63				keys := make([]string, 0, len(m))
64				for k := range m {
65					if r, _ := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(k); unicode.IsUpper(r) {
66						keys = append(keys, k)
67					}
68				}
69				sort.Strings(keys)
70				return rawText + "\x00" + t.String() + "\x00" + strings.Join(keys, "\x01")
71			}

81	func parsedTemplate(text *Text) (*template.Template, error) {

85		key := templateCacheKey(text.template, text.context)

92		text.patchTemplate()

95		tmpl, err := template.New("cache").Funcs(funcMap()).Parse(text.template)

98		}

101		actual, _ := parsedTemplates.LoadOrStore(key, tmpl)
102		return actual.(*template.Template), nil
103	}

107		if err != nil {

111

115		if err != nil {

123		// https://github.com/golang/go/issues/24963

Base directory for this skill: D:\oh-my-posh\.claude\skills\conventional-commit

# Conventional Commit

## Commit Message Structure

```text
<type>(<scope>): <description>

[optional body]

[optional footer(s)]
```

### Types

| Type       | When to use                                            |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `feat`     | A new feature                                          |
| `fix`      | A bug fix                                              |
| `docs`     | Documentation changes, no code                         |
| `style`    | Formatting, missing semicolons, etc. (no logic change) |
| `refactor` | Code change that is neither a fix nor a feature        |
| `perf`     | Performance improvement                                |
| `test`     | Adding or correcting tests                             |
| `ci`       | CI configuration changes                               |
| `chore`    | Maintenance tasks (updating deps, tooling, etc.)       |
| `revert`   | Reverts a previous commit                              |

Append `!` after the type/scope to signal a **breaking change**: `feat!:` or `feat(api)!:`
When a change breaks existing behavior, **both markers are mandatory**: the `!` suffix on the type
**and** the `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer. They always appear together — never one without the other.

### Scope

Optional. Use the name of the area affected, e.g., `segment`, `cache`, `config`, `ui`.
Omit when the change is truly cross-cutting.

### Description

- Required. One short imperative sentence, no period at the end. The full header line (type +
  scope + description) must be **72 characters or fewer**. Aim for **50 characters or fewer for
  the description itself** — this almost always keeps the full header within budget regardless
  of type and scope length.
- Use the imperative mood: "add", not "added" or "adds". Never past tense or present-third-person:
  ✗ `added`, `fixed`, `bumped`, `implemented` → ✓ `add`, `fix`, `bump`, `implement`.
- **Never mirror the input's phrasing.** If the request uses past-tense words (`updated`, `added`,
  `bumped`, `was removed`, `got regenerated`), convert them to imperative before writing the
  description: `update`, `add`, `bump`, `remove`, `regenerate`.

### Body

Optional. Add context about _why_ the change was made, not _what_. The diff shows that.
Wrap at 72 characters.

### Footer

Use for:

- `BREAKING CHANGE: <description>` (required when `!` is used; explains the break).
- Issue references: `Closes #123`, `Fixes #456`.
- Co-authors: `Co-Authored-By: Name <email>`.

## Workflow

1. Run `git status` to review changed files.
2. Run `git diff` and `git diff --cached` to inspect staged and unstaged changes.
3. Identify the **type** from the table above. Ask yourself: does this change **remove, rename, or
   alter existing behavior** that callers depend on? If yes → it is a breaking change: use `!`
   after the type/scope **and** add a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer. Both markers are always required
   together.
4. Identify the **scope** from the files/area changed.
5. Write a short **description** in the imperative mood.
6. Add a **body** if the _why_ needs explanation.
7. Add a **footer** for breaking changes or issue references.
8. Stage the relevant files explicitly (avoid `git add -A`).
9. Commit with a message that preserves multi-line formatting when body/footer are present.

## Examples

```text
feat(segment): add Ramadan segment with Aladhan API
fix(cache): always store mod time
docs(readme): update installation instructions
refactor(config): simplify option parsing logic
chore(deps): bump github.com/shirou/gopsutil/v4
feat(segment)!: rename template property StartTime to Start

BREAKING CHANGE: template strings using .StartTime must be updated to .Start
```

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] Type is one of the allowed values in .commitlintrc.yml
- [ ] The commit message respects the rules defined in .commitlintrc.yml
- [ ] Scope (if present) reflects the actual area changed
- [ ] Description is imperative mood, no trailing period
- [ ] Full header line (type + scope + description) is 72 characters or fewer
- [ ] Both `!` after type/scope **and** `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer are present whenever the change breaks existing behavior
- [ ] No sensitive files staged (.env, credentials, etc.)

cd D:/oh-my-posh && git add src/config/segment.go src/config/segment_cache_test.go && git commit --amend -m "refactor(config): use generic cache for SegmentWriter

Store cached segment writers as gob-encoded snapshots instead of
JSON strings. Restoring decodes the snapshot into the writer
initialized by MapSegmentWithWriter, so its unexported runtime state
(env, options) survives. Replacing the writer instance dropped that
state, and any method relying on it panicked after a restore,
crashing the prompt process until the cache entry expired.

The snapshot also keeps cached state immutable: storing the live
writer would share one mutable object between render passes and the
cache. Legacy JSON entries are deleted on first read and re-cached
in the new format.
